reason: Both answers correctly note model quality was never a durable moat and locate defensibility in workflow depth, proprietary data loops, distribution/trust, domain-specific evaluation, and cost/latency engineering. Both value the six-month lead near zero. They are nearly identical in quality. Answer A adds a concrete self-diagnostic question and a historical analogy (databases, cloud, mobile) plus explicit dos-and-donts, giving slightly more actionable texture. Answer B is a touch more concise with a good closing reframe but adds nothing beyond A. Difference is marginal but A edges ahead.
